svg.text: All three trials have p > 0.05 — but mean completely different things
The p-value cannot distinguish these. Only the CI can.

g.text: NEGATIVE — "Clinically meaningful benefit is ruled out"
NEUTRAL — "No meaningful difference in either direction"
Key: p > 0.05 has three faces — only the CI width reveals which one

svg.text: Wide CI → underpowered / inconclusive | Narrow CI past MCID → negative | Narrow CI at null → neutral
Saying "no significant difference" for ALL three is the most common error in medicine
